claim: The 'design tree' concept from software architecture (exploring all branches of decisions before committing to code) is a powerful framework for interviewing LLMs about complex features
actionable: When planning a feature, use the design tree framework to walk through all decision branches with Claude Code, resolving dependencies between decisions before implementing
source_title: 5 Claude Code skills I use every single day

claim: The 'grill-me' skill (forcing deep questioning before implementation) prevents Claude Code from suggesting half-baked plans and reaches shared understanding faster
actionable: Create a grill-me skill that forces Claude Code to ask 15-20 clarifying questions about every design aspect before suggesting implementation
source_title: 5 Claude Code skills I use every single day

claim: Converting PRDs (Problem-Solution-User Stories) into vertical slices (thin slices that cut through all layers) prevents horizontal refactoring and surfaces integration risks early
actionable: When breaking PRDs into tasks, create vertical slices that integrate new components end-to-end rather than implementing one layer at a time
source_title: 5 Claude Code skills I use every single day

claim: Test-driven development (red-green-refactor) with Claude Code produces higher quality code than free-form implementation, even when Claude Code is reluctant to refactor its own code
actionable: Force Claude Code to write failing tests first, then implement to pass, then refactor by using the TDD skill in your prompts
source_title: 5 Claude Code skills I use every single day

claim: Clear module boundaries and interface definitions make TDD easier for Claude Code because test boundaries become unambiguous
actionable: Before asking Claude Code to write tests, ensure your codebase has clear module boundaries with well-defined interfaces
source_title: 5 Claude Code skills I use every single day

claim: Regularly refactoring codebase architecture improves Claude Code output quality because the AI navigates cleaner module structures more effectively
actionable: Run architecture refactoring passes monthly to deepen shallow modules and establish clearer boundaries so Claude Code understands your codebase better
source_title: 5 Claude Code skills I use every single day

claim: Spawning 3 subagents to propose radically different interface designs before choosing one is better than asking Claude Code to pick the 'best' design directly
actionable: When refactoring, ask Claude Code to generate 3 completely different architectural approaches before selecting or combining elements from each
source_title: 5 Claude Code skills I use every single day

claim: Skills don't need to be long to be impactful; choosing the right words at the right time is more important than comprehensiveness
actionable: Write skills as concise, focused instructions (even 3 sentences) rather than exhaustive documentation; prioritize clarity over completeness
source_title: 5 Claude Code skills I use every single day
